message:
test(core): add 13 write-path edge-case tests and apply rustfmt across 4 crates

Write-path tests (bd-43w8) in ffs-core cover:
- Sequential appends growing file size
- Partial overwrite of middle bytes
- Sparse holes reading as zeros past first block boundary
- Truncate-to-zero then rewrite
- Rename over existing file preserving source inode data
- rmdir on empty directory
- Hard link sharing data after overwrite via original inode
- Rename directory across parent directories
- setattr utimes updating atime/mtime to specific epoch
- fallocate PUNCH_HOLE zeroing middle region while preserving size
- Mass file creation (50 files) forcing directory block expansion
- Symlink readlink resolution and rejection of write to symlink inode

Formatting cleanup (rustfmt):
- ffs-cli: collapse multi-line try_parse_from arrays onto single lines
  in cli_parses_dump_dir_command_with_hex and
  cli_rejects_mkfs_non_numeric_size_mb tests
- ffs-dir: expand write_entry call with usize::MAX-2 offset to
  multi-line, replace `as u32` casts with `u32::try_from().expect()`
  for safer narrowing in add_entry and parse_dir_block test helpers
- ffs-xattr: reflow entry_index assertions, collapse short set_xattr
  calls, and simplify list_xattrs assertion vec formatting

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
